name: meeting-exec-pod
description: Turns meeting transcripts into tracked action items with AI agents

tables.name: decisions
tables.description: Decisions made in meetings. Used by the contradiction_detector agent to surface logical conflicts between new decisions and historical ones.

columns.name: id
columns.description: UUID generated at creation

columns.name: text
columns.description: Full text of the decision as extracted by the AI agent

columns.name: meeting_id
columns.description: References meetings.id this decision came from

columns.name: meeting_title
columns.description: Denormalized meeting title for quick display

columns.name: created_at
columns.description: When this decision was recorded

indexes.description: For chronological ordering in contradiction checks

tables.name: meetings
tables.description: Log of processed meeting transcripts with aggregated counts.

columns.name: id
columns.description: UUID generated at creation

columns.name: title
columns.description: Meeting title, derived from notes or user-supplied

columns.name: raw_notes
columns.description: Full original transcript or notes text

columns.name: task_count
columns.description: Number of tasks created from this meeting

columns.name: decision_count
columns.description: Number of decisions recorded from this meeting

columns.name: processed_at
columns.description: When extraction was completed and records committed

tables.name: tasks
tables.description: Action items extracted from meeting transcripts.

columns.name: id
columns.description: UUID generated at creation

columns.name: title
columns.description: Short imperative task title

columns.name: description
columns.description: One sentence of context from the meeting

columns.name: owner
columns.description: Team member name (references team_members.name)

columns.name: deadline
columns.description: ISO date YYYY-MM-DD or null if not set

columns.name: status
columns.description: Current execution state

columns.name: priority
columns.description: Task urgency level

columns.name: meeting_id
columns.description: References meetings.id this task came from

columns.name: source_meeting_title
columns.description: Denormalized meeting title for quick display

columns.name: created_at
columns.description: When record was inserted

columns.name: followup_sent
columns.description: Whether a deadline reminder has been dispatched

columns.name: blocked_reason
columns.description: Free-text explanation of what is blocking this task

columns.name: blocked_at
columns.description: When the blocker was reported

columns.name: escalated
columns.description: Whether a blocker escalation reminder has been sent

columns.name: recurrence_count
columns.description: How many times this task (or a similar one) has appeared across meetings

columns.name: first_seen_meeting_id
columns.description: The meeting_id where this task was first created

columns.name: is_recurring
columns.description: True if this task was detected as a duplicate of an existing task

columns.name: completion_note
columns.description: Employee-written description of what was actually done to complete the task

columns.name: completion_evidence
columns.description: Link to PR, Notion doc, Loom recording, or screenshot proving completion

columns.name: completion_verified
columns.description: True if the AI verifier (completion-verifier agent) approved the completion note

columns.name: completed_at
columns.description: When the task was marked done after passing verification

columns.name: rejected_count
columns.description: Number of times the employee tried to mark done but was rejected by AI or founder

columns.name: cg_escalated
columns.description: True if this task was escalated due to repeated completion rejections (rejected_count >= 2)

columns.name: blocked_by
columns.description: Array of task IDs that must be completed before this task can be started or marked done.

columns.name: history
columns.description: Audit trail of state changes. Array of objects {status, timestamp, by, note}

indexes.description: For verified completion stats in insights bar
For finding disputed tasks needing founder attention

tables.name: team_members
tables.description: Core team roster. Seed rows pre-loaded at pod init.

columns.name: name
columns.description: Full name, used as owner reference in tasks

columns.name: role
columns.description: Job title or function

columns.name: email
columns.description: Contact email for follow-up messages

agents.name: completion-verifier
agents.description: Reviews a task completion note written by an employee and decides whether it credibly demonstrates the task was actually finished. Prevents "click Done and walk away" from reaching the founder as false signal.
agents.instruction:
You are a strict but fair task completion reviewer for a startup execution system.

You receive:
  - task_title: what the task was
  - task_priority: high | medium | low
  - deadline: ISO date the task was due
  - days_overdue: positive = overdue, negative = early
  - completion_note: what the employee wrote about what they did
  - evidence_link: PR / Notion doc / Loom URL (may be empty)

Your job: decide if this completion note CREDIBLY demonstrates the task is done.

A note is INSUFFICIENT (verdict: "needs_more") if:
  - It's vague: "done", "fixed it", "completed", "all good", "ok", "finished"
  - It doesn't mention WHAT specifically was changed, fixed, or delivered
  - It contradicts the task (task: fix auth bug → note: "updated docs")
  - The note is under 30 words with no technical specifics
  - Priority is "high" AND no evidence_link provided AND note has no technical detail

A note is REJECTED (verdict: "rejected") if:
  - The completion note describes a DIFFERENT task entirely
  - The note actively contradicts the task (task: "ship feature X", note: "I decided not to do X")
  - The employee is claiming done for something that logically can't be done yet

A note is APPROVED (verdict: "approved") if:
  - It clearly describes what action was taken
  - It mentions the outcome or result (e.g. "tested with 3 clients", "PR merged", "doc published")
  - High priority tasks: evidence_link provided OR note is detailed and technical (30+ words)

Output STRICT JSON only, no preamble, no markdown fences:
{
  "verdict": "approved" | "needs_more" | "rejected",
  "reason": "one sentence explaining the verdict",
  "suggestion": "specific, actionable thing to add (only when needs_more or rejected)",
  "confidence": 0.0-1.0
}

Examples:
  Task: "Fix auth bug" | Note: "fixed" → needs_more (too vague)
  Task: "Fix auth bug" | Note: "Resolved Auth0 callback URL mismatch. Updated in dashboard and tested login with 3 enterprise accounts." → approved
  Task: "Fix auth bug" | Note: "Updated the landing page copy" → rejected (wrong task)
  Task: "Write proposal" | Note: "Wrote the proposal" → needs_more (no outcome described)
  Task: "Write proposal" | Note: "Drafted 3-page proposal, sent to client via email, awaiting response." → approved

task_title.description: The task that was supposedly completed

deadline.description: ISO date the task was due

completion_note.description: Employee-written description of what was done

evidence_link.description: Optional PR link, Notion doc, Loom URL

agents.name: contradiction-detector
agents.description: Receives new decisions just extracted from a meeting and existing decisions from the decisions table. Identifies logical conflicts — cases where two decisions are on the same topic but point in opposite directions.
agents.instruction:
You are a decision consistency analyst for a startup execution system.
You receive two inputs:
  - new_decisions: list of decision strings just extracted from today's meeting
  - existing_decisions: list of {text, meeting_title, created_at} from past meetings

Your job: compare EVERY new decision against EVERY existing one.
A contradiction is when two decisions are logically incompatible on the same topic.
Examples:
  - "Going with 3-step onboarding flow" vs "Add a verification step before payment" → CONFLICT
  - "Cut marketing spend 30%" vs "Increase marketing investment" → CONFLICT
  - "No backend changes needed" vs "We need backend API changes for this feature" → CONFLICT
Non-examples (NOT conflicts):
  - "Ship by July 10th" vs "Ship mobile app by August" → different products
  - "Hire 2 AEs" vs "Hire a designer" → different roles, no conflict

Output STRICT JSON only, no preamble, no markdown:
{
  "contradictions": [
    {
      "new_decision": "...",
      "conflicts_with": "...",
      "from_meeting": "...",
      "severity": "high|medium"
    }
  ],
  "clean": ["decisions with no conflicts"]
}

Severity guide:
  - "high": directly blocks execution or reverses a shipped decision
  - "medium": creates confusion or rework but not an immediate blocker

new_decisions.description: Decisions just extracted from the current meeting

existing_decisions.description: All historical decisions from the decisions table

agents.name: action-item-extractor
agents.description: Extracts every action item, decision, and follow-up meeting from raw meeting notes or timestamped transcripts. Outputs structured JSON ready for validation.
agents.instruction:
You are a meeting execution assistant. You receive raw meeting notes (may be
transcripts with timestamps or clean notes). You also receive a list of team
members and pre-resolved dates. Your job:

1. Extract EVERY action item, commitment, and "to-do" from the notes. If
   something sounds like work someone needs to do, it IS a task — even if
   stated casually.
2. Extract every DECISION made (use a separate "decisions" array).
3. Extract any scheduled FOLLOW-UP MEETINGS.

For EACH action item, output:
- title: Short, imperative task title (e.g. "Finalize Figma files and hand off to engineering")
- description: One sentence of context
- owner: EXACT name from the team members list. If unclear, use "UNASSIGNED"
- deadline: ISO date string (YYYY-MM-DD). Use the resolved_dates map provided.
  If no deadline found, use "NO_DEADLINE"
- priority: "high" (blocking, revenue-impacting, or from leadership),
  "medium" (normal sprint work), "low" (nice to have, vague)

Output STRICT JSON with no preamble, no markdown fences, no commentary:
{
  "tasks": [
    {
      "title": "...",
      "description": "...",
      "owner": "...",
      "deadline": "...",
      "priority": "..."
    }
  ],
  "decisions": ["...", "..."],
  "follow_up_meetings": ["..."]
}

notes_text.description: Raw transcript or meeting notes to process

team_members.description: Current team roster for owner resolution

resolved_dates.description: Map of relative date phrases to ISO dates, e.g. {"Thursday": "2025-07-03", "next Tuesday": "2025-07-08"}

agents.name: followup-drafter
agents.description: Writes short, friendly Slack reminders for upcoming task deadlines. Tone: casual peer-to-peer, not corporate.
agents.instruction:
You write short, friendly Slack messages reminding a team member about an
upcoming task deadline. Keep it to 2-3 sentences. Mention the task, the
deadline, and ask if they need help. Do NOT be formal. Do NOT use corporate
jargon. Write like a colleague, not a manager.

Input will include the task details and the owner's name. Output ONLY the
message body — no subject line, no "Hi [name]:" salutation, just the message.

task.description: Task record to generate reminder for

owner.description: Team member who owns this task

output_schema.description: The Slack message body, 2-3 sentences, casual tone

agents.name: hinglish-cleaner
agents.description: Cleans raw Hinglish or code-switched meeting notes into clear English.
agents.instruction:
You receive raw Hinglish or code-switched meeting notes. Your job is to output 
clean, grammatically correct English while preserving names, dates, and technical 
terms exactly as spoken.

Output strict JSON only, with no preamble or markdown wrapping.

cleaned.description: The translated and cleaned meeting notes in clear English.

agents.name: recurring-checker
agents.description: Checks if two task titles describe the exact same underlying action item.
agents.instruction:
You are an assistant checking for duplicate tasks. You receive two task descriptions.
Your job is to determine if they represent the exact same underlying action/deliverable.

Example of SAME:
  - "Fix the auth bug" and "Resolve authentication error"
  - "Send email to investors" and "Email the investor update"

Example of DIFFERENT:
  - "Fix the auth bug" and "Fix the styling bug on login page"
  - "Update onboarding docs" and "Update API docs"

Output strict JSON only:
{
  "is_same": boolean
}

agents.name: voice-note-transcriber
agents.description: Cleans raw voice note transcripts — informal, fragmented, Hindi-English mixed ("Hinglish"), no timestamps — into structured meeting notes that the action-item-extractor agent can process. Preserves all commitments, deadlines, and decisions even if stated casually.
agents.instruction:
You are a meeting notes formatter specializing in Indian startup communication.
You receive raw WhatsApp voice note transcripts. These may be:
  - Informal and fragmented
  - Mixed Hindi-English ("Hinglish")
  - Lacking punctuation or timestamps
  - Using casual shorthand (e.g. "kal tak", "EOD", "ASAP")

Your job: clean the transcript into structured English meeting notes that
preserve every commitment, deadline, and decision — even casually stated ones.

Rules:
  - Identify WHO is responsible for WHAT by WHEN
  - Translate Hinglish phrases to English equivalents:
      "kal tak" → "by tomorrow"
      "karna hai" → "needs to be done"
      "bhejni hai" → "needs to send"
      "wala" → referring to (context-dependent)
      "abhi" → "right now / immediately"
      "seedha" → "directly"
  - Output clean English meeting notes. NO JSON. Plain text only.
  - Format: "Person: Task by Deadline." — one line per task.
  - If no deadline mentioned, omit the deadline clause.
  - Preserve proper nouns (names, product names) as-is.

Example input:
  "haan toh arjun wala bug fix karna hai by friday, aur priya ko email
   bhejni hai before release, rahul investor ko deck bhejna hai next tuesday"

Example output:
  "Arjun: Fix the bug by Friday.
   Priya: Send the email before the release date.
   Rahul: Send the updated deck to the investor by next Tuesday."

raw_transcript.description: Raw voice note text — may be Hinglish, informal, fragmented

output_schema.description: Cleaned, structured English meeting notes ready for extraction

workflows.name: blocker-check
workflows.description: Daily workflow that identifies tasks stuck in "in_progress" for more than 2 days without escalation, drafts a reminder message via the followup-drafter agent, and requests human approval before sending.

steps.description: Find tasks stuck in progress for more than 2 days, not yet escalated
For each stuck task, draft a follow-up reminder message
Draft a reminder message asking about status and blockers

input.context: This task has been in progress for {{ (now() - task.created_at).days }} days. We want to check if the owner is blocked or needs help.

steps.title: Stuck Task Escalation — {{ steps.find_stuck.output.rows | length }} task(s)
steps.description: Review drafted reminder messages before sending. Select which tasks to escalate and which to skip.
Mark approved tasks as escalated to prevent re-sending next day

workflows.name: deadline-check
workflows.description: Daily job: find tasks due tomorrow or overdue where no follow-up has been sent, draft Slack reminders via AI, get human approval, then mark sent.

trigger.description: Runs every morning to catch upcoming deadlines

steps.description: Find tasks approaching deadline that haven't been reminded yet
For each due task, draft a Slack reminder using the AI agent
Mark followup_sent so we don't re-remind

workflows.name: process-meeting
workflows.description: End-to-end pipeline: transcript → parsed entities → AI extraction → contradiction check → recurring task detection → validation → human review → committed task + decision records.

notes_text.description: Raw meeting transcript or notes to process

meeting_title.description: Human-readable title for the meeting record

steps.description: Resolve relative dates and identify team members in the notes
AI extraction of tasks, decisions, and follow-up meetings
Compare newly extracted decisions against all historical decisions
Split extracted tasks into valid vs. flagged for human review

steps.title: Review Extracted Tasks
steps.description: Found {{ steps.validate.output.total }} tasks. {{ steps.validate.output.needs_attention }} need your attention. {{ steps.check_contradictions.output.contradictions | length }} decision conflict(s) detected.
Commit approved tasks, decisions, and meeting record to database

workflows.name: voice-note-intake
workflows.description: Processes a raw WhatsApp voice note transcript (Hinglish OK) through the voice-note-transcriber agent to clean it up, then feeds the cleaned notes into the full process-meeting workflow. Supports manual trigger and future WhatsApp webhook integration.

webhook.description: Future: WhatsApp Business API can POST voice note transcriptions here. Payload: { "from": "+91XXXXXXXXXX", "transcript": "..." }

raw_transcript.description: Raw voice note text — Hinglish or informal English OK

sender.description: WhatsApp sender name or phone (for context)

steps.description: Clean and structure the raw Hinglish voice note transcript
Feed the cleaned transcript into the full extraction pipeline — reusing parse_entities → extract → validate → human_review → create_records

functions.name: create_task_records
functions.source:
"""
create_task_records.py

Commits approved tasks to the tasks table, decisions to the decisions table,
and creates a meeting record. Called after human approval step in process_meeting workflow.

Input:
  approved_tasks (list[dict]): Human-reviewed and approved task objects
  meeting_title (str): Title of the source meeting
  raw_notes (str): Original meeting notes/transcript
  decisions (list[str]): Decisions extracted from the meeting

Output:
  {
    "meeting_id": str,
    "task_ids": list[str],
    "task_count": int,
    "decision_count": int,
    "recurring_count": int,
  }

Note: actual DB writes handled by Lemma runtime via `db` context injected at
execution time. This module defines the transformation logic; the runtime
handles persistence. In mock mode, the frontend handles this locally.
"""

import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timezone


def _word_overlap(title_a: str, title_b: str) -> float:
    """
    Simple word-overlap similarity for recurring task detection.
    Returns ratio of common non-trivial words to max title word count.
    Threshold 0.45 → treat as recurring.
    """
    stop = {"the", "a", "an", "to", "for", "in", "on", "with", "and", "or",
            "of", "is", "are", "was", "were", "be", "been", "has", "have",
            "new", "this", "that", "from", "by", "at", "it"}
    wa = [w for w in title_a.lower().split() if len(w) > 2 and w not in stop]
    wb = [w for w in title_b.lower().split() if len(w) > 2 and w not in stop]
    if not wa or not wb:
        return 0.0
    set_a = set(wa)
    common = sum(1 for w in wb if w in set_a)
    return common / max(len(wa), len(wb))


def run(
    approved_tasks: list[dict],
    meeting_title: str,
    raw_notes: str = "",
    decisions: list[str] | None = None,
    existing_tasks: list[dict] | None = None,   # injected by runtime from tasks table
) -> dict:
    decisions = decisions or []
    existing_tasks = existing_tasks or []
    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()

    meeting_id = str(uuid.uuid4())

    # ── Meeting record ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    meeting_record = {
        "id": meeting_id,
        "title": meeting_title,
        "raw_notes": raw_notes,
        "task_count": len(approved_tasks),
        "decision_count": len(decisions),
        "processed_at": now,
    }

    # ── Recurring task detection ──────────────────────────────────────────────
    SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD = 0.45
    task_records = []
    recurring_updates = []   # existing task IDs to increment recurrence_count on

    for task in approved_tasks:
        matched_existing = None
        for et in existing_tasks:
            if et.get("status") == "done":
                continue
            if _word_overlap(task.get("title", ""), et.get("title", "")) >= SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD:
                matched_existing = et
                break

        if matched_existing:
            # Don't create duplicate — update the existing task
            new_count = (matched_existing.get("recurrence_count") or 1) + 1
            update = {
                "id": matched_existing["id"],
                "recurrence_count": new_count,
                "is_recurring": True,
                "priority": "high" if new_count >= 3 else matched_existing.get("priority", "medium"),
            }
            recurring_updates.append(update)
        else:
            task_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
            record = {
                "id": task_id,
                "title": task.get("title", ""),
                "description": task.get("description", ""),
                "owner": task.get("owner", "UNASSIGNED"),
                "deadline": task.get("deadline") if task.get("deadline") not in ("NO_DEADLINE", "", None) else None,
                "status": "todo",
                "priority": task.get("priority", "medium"),
                "meeting_id": meeting_id,
                "source_meeting_title": meeting_title,
                "created_at": now,
                "followup_sent": False,
                # New fields with defaults
                "blocked_reason": None,
                "blocked_at": None,
                "escalated": False,
                "recurrence_count": 1,
                "first_seen_meeting_id": meeting_id,
                "is_recurring": False,
            }
            task_records.append(record)

    # ── Decision records ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    decision_records = []
    for text in decisions:
        decision_records.append({
            "id": str(uuid.uuid4()),
            "text": text,
            "meeting_id": meeting_id,
            "meeting_title": meeting_title,
            "created_at": now,
        })

    return {
        "meeting_id": meeting_id,
        "meeting_record": meeting_record,
        "task_ids": [r["id"] for r in task_records],
        "task_records": task_records,
        "decision_records": decision_records,
        "recurring_updates": recurring_updates,
        "task_count": len(task_records),
        "decision_count": len(decision_records),
        "recurring_count": len(recurring_updates),
    }

functions.name: parse_entities
functions.source:
"""
parse_entities.py

Resolves relative date phrases in meeting notes to ISO dates,
identifies mentioned team members, and detects transcript format.

Input:
  notes_text (str): Raw meeting notes or transcript
  team_names (list[str]): Known team member names

Output:
  {
    "resolved_dates": dict[str, str],   # phrase -> ISO date
    "mentioned_team_members": list[str],
    "has_timestamps": bool
  }
"""

import re
from datetime import date, timedelta


def run(notes_text: str, team_names: list[str]) -> dict:
    today = date.today()

    resolved_dates = _resolve_dates(notes_text, today)
    mentioned = _find_members(notes_text, team_names)
    has_timestamps = bool(re.search(r'\[\d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}\]', notes_text))

    return {
        "resolved_dates": resolved_dates,
        "mentioned_team_members": mentioned,
        "has_timestamps": has_timestamps,
    }


def _resolve_dates(text: str, today: date) -> dict[str, str]:
    resolved = {}

    # today / tomorrow
    resolved["today"] = today.isoformat()
    resolved["tomorrow"] = (today + timedelta(days=1)).isoformat()
    resolved["day after tomorrow"] = (today + timedelta(days=2)).isoformat()

    # weekday names → next occurrence (including today)
    weekday_names = ["monday", "tuesday", "wednesday", "thursday", "friday", "saturday", "sunday"]
    for i, wd in enumerate(weekday_names):
        days_ahead = (i - today.weekday()) % 7
        # if today IS that weekday, return today (not +7)
        resolved[wd] = (today + timedelta(days=days_ahead)).isoformat()
        resolved[f"by {wd}"] = resolved[wd]
        resolved[f"next {wd}"] = (today + timedelta(days=(days_ahead or 7))).isoformat()
        if days_ahead == 0:
            resolved[f"next {wd}"] = (today + timedelta(days=7)).isoformat()

    # "this week" → coming Friday
    days_to_friday = (4 - today.weekday()) % 7
    resolved["this week"] = (today + timedelta(days=days_to_friday or 7)).isoformat()
    resolved["end of week"] = resolved["this week"]

    # "next week" → Monday of next week
    days_to_next_monday = (7 - today.weekday()) % 7 or 7
    next_monday = today + timedelta(days=days_to_next_monday)
    resolved["next week"] = next_monday.isoformat()

    # "end of month"
    if today.month == 12:
        eom = date(today.year + 1, 1, 1) - timedelta(days=1)
    else:
        eom = date(today.year, today.month + 1, 1) - timedelta(days=1)
    resolved["end of month"] = eom.isoformat()

    # "before July 10" → July 9 (current or next year)
    before_matches = re.findall(
        r'before\s+(january|february|march|april|may|june|july|august|september|october|november|december)\s+(\d{1,2})',
        text, re.IGNORECASE
    )
    month_map = {
        "january": 1, "february": 2, "march": 3, "april": 4,
        "may": 5, "june": 6, "july": 7, "august": 8,
        "september": 9, "october": 10, "november": 11, "december": 12,
    }
    for mon_str, day_str in before_matches:
        m = month_map[mon_str.lower()]
        d = int(day_str)
        target = date(today.year, m, d) - timedelta(days=1)
        if target < today:
            target = date(today.year + 1, m, d) - timedelta(days=1)
        key = f"before {mon_str.capitalize()} {day_str}"
        resolved[key] = target.isoformat()
        resolved[key.lower()] = target.isoformat()

    # explicit "July 10" style → parse inline dates too
    month_day_matches = re.findall(
        r'(january|february|march|april|may|june|july|august|september|october|november|december)\s+(\d{1,2})(?:st|nd|rd|th)?',
        text, re.IGNORECASE
    )
    for mon_str, day_str in month_day_matches:
        m = month_map[mon_str.lower()]
        d = int(day_str)
        target = date(today.year, m, d)
        if target < today:
            target = date(today.year + 1, m, d)
        key = f"{mon_str.capitalize()} {day_str}"
        resolved[key] = target.isoformat()

    # "end of August" style
    end_of_month_matches = re.findall(
        r'end\s+of\s+(january|february|march|april|may|june|july|august|september|october|november|december)',
        text, re.IGNORECASE
    )
    for mon_str in end_of_month_matches:
        m = month_map[mon_str.lower()]
        year = today.year if m >= today.month else today.year + 1
        if m == 12:
            eom_target = date(year + 1, 1, 1) - timedelta(days=1)
        else:
            eom_target = date(year, m + 1, 1) - timedelta(days=1)
        key = f"end of {mon_str.capitalize()}"
        resolved[key] = eom_target.isoformat()
        resolved[key.lower()] = eom_target.isoformat()

    return resolved


def _find_members(text: str, team_names: list[str]) -> list[str]:
    mentioned = []
    for name in team_names:
        if re.search(r'\b' + re.escape(name) + r'\b', text, re.IGNORECASE):
            mentioned.append(name)
    return mentioned

functions.name: validate_tasks
functions.source:
"""
validate_tasks.py

Validates extracted tasks for required fields (owner, deadline).
Splits into valid (ready to commit) and flagged (needs human attention).

Input:
  extracted_tasks (list[dict]): Raw task objects from extractor agent

Output:
  {
    "valid_tasks": list[dict],
    "flagged_tasks": list[dict],   # each has "_flags" key
    "total": int,
    "ready_count": int,
    "needs_attention": int,
  }
"""


def run(extracted_tasks: list[dict]) -> dict:
    valid_tasks = []
    flagged_tasks = []

    for task in extracted_tasks:
        flags = _validate(task)
        if flags:
            task_copy = dict(task)
            task_copy["_flags"] = flags
            flagged_tasks.append(task_copy)
        else:
            valid_tasks.append(task)

    return {
        "valid_tasks": valid_tasks,
        "flagged_tasks": flagged_tasks,
        "total": len(extracted_tasks),
        "ready_count": len(valid_tasks),
        "needs_attention": len(flagged_tasks),
    }


def _validate(task: dict) -> list[str]:
    flags = []

    owner = task.get("owner", "").strip()
    if not owner or owner.upper() == "UNASSIGNED":
        flags.append("NO_OWNER")

    deadline = task.get("deadline", "").strip()
    if not deadline or deadline.upper() == "NO_DEADLINE":
        flags.append("NO_DEADLINE")

    return flags
